ASA Jan 28 '26, 7:10 PM in 1h NBA |
                                                                        Lakers vs Cavs Play on: OVER 235½ -110 ASA NBA play on OVER 235.5 LA Lakers at Cleveland Cavaliers, 7:10pm ET - Two of the NBA’s top 5-scorers face off in this game with Luka Doncic leading the league with a 33.8 scoring average while Donovan Mitchell of the Cavs is putting up 29.5ppg, 5th most. We are expecting plenty of points in this game which should eclipse the 240 barrier with a Cavs offense that is 10th in offensive efficiency squaring off against a Lakers D that is 20th in defensive efficiency. The Cavs defense has slipped this season, ranking 14th in dEFF after ranking 8th last season. Tonight the Cavs will be missing reigning defensive player of the year Mobley and could potentially be without another solid defender in Garland (listed as ?). The Lakers are 8th in offensive efficiency averaging 1.180-points per possession and rank 3rd on the season in eFG% at 56.7%. The Cavaliers are 7th in the league in pace of play this season and the Lakers are playing faster than normal in their last 5 games. It all adds up to a higher scoring game in Cleveland tonight. Stephen Nover Jan 28 '26, 7:10 PM in 1h NBA |
                                                                        Bulls vs Pacers Play on: Bulls -130 at betus A bad 129-118 home loss to the Lakers two days ago, obscures how well the Bulls have recently been playing. Chicago had won four in a row until that defeat. These victories came against three strong teams, too, - Celtics, Timberwolves and Clippers. Chicago held its last five opponents to an average of 110 points, 10 points below its season defensive average, before the loss to the Lakers. The Bulls should not lack motivation to get back to their winning ways. They have double revenge against the Pacers having lost twice to Indiana this season. Chicago is 23-23. Indiana is 11-36, which is the worst record in the NBA. Indiana ranks second-to-last in scoring averaging 110.2 points a game. The Pacers are also the worst shooting team in the NBA making just 44.3 percent of their shots from the floor. It is a bad situational spot for the Pacers, too. This is their first home game since finishing a five-game, 10-day road trip that concluded this past Monday night with a 132-116 loss to the Hawks. The Pacers have lost eight of their last 11 home games. The Bulls are a tricky team. But it shouldn't be too much to ask of them to just beat the Pacers by backing them on the money line. (Editor's note: Stephen Nover has his strongest Atlantic 10 Conference play of the season today in addition to this free NBA selection.) Released on Jan 28 at 12:22 pm View Archive CLICK HERE FOR PREMIUM PICK OPTIONS
